title: Blakea tetramera
intro: Blakea tetramera is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae, native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Blakea tetramera include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Blakea tetramera is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine in the Andean region.
